company: Momentum Ventures Studio LLC (Accelerator / Early Stage Investor)
nda_type: One-Way (We are Receiving Party — our IP disclosed to accelerator)
summary: CRITICAL — Do not sign. Document titled 'NDA' is structurally an IP acquisition instrument. Momentum Ventures Studio is an accelerator presenting a routine NDA as part of an 'application review' process. Three critical defects that make this document dangerous for any technology company: (1) Section 4 contains a 'work for hire' provision stating that any technology demonstrated, disclosed, or developed during the accelerator engagement is deemed a 'work made for hire' under 17 U.S.C. §101 — software that qualifies is immediately owned by Momentum; (2) Section 5.2 includes a broad assignment clause: 'Company irrevocably assigns to Momentum Ventures all right, title, and interest in any improvement, adaptation, or derivative of Disclosed Technology' — this is a present-tense IP assignment, not a future obligation; (3) Governing law is California, which under Cal. Lab. Code §2870 voids IP assignments that conflict with employee prior inventions, but the accelerator's assignment clause does not carve out §2870-protected inventions. Additionally, perpetual obligations, three missing clauses, and no compelled disclosure carve-out. This document should be rejected without counter-proposal — engage own counsel to draft a mutual NDA with no IP provisions.

playbook_results.deviation: Definition includes 'technology, source code, algorithms, business models, customer data, and any concept or idea discussed during the Program, including information disclosed orally without written confirmation.' The absence of any written confirmation requirement for oral disclosures means that any verbal discussion in a pitch meeting is immediately captured as Confidential Information — with no time limit, no scope limit, and no written record required.

The document inverts the typical NDA structure: we (the company disclosing our technology) are designated the 'Receiving Party' for the purposes of section 5's IP assignment — while Momentum is the 'Disclosing Party' providing 'program resources.' This inversion means Receiving Party obligations in section 3 apply to us (standard confidentiality) while section 5's IP assignment obligation also applies to us. Both directions of obligation flow against our company.

Confidentiality obligations: perpetual ('for so long as the information retains commercial value'). IP assignment in section 5.2: permanent and irrevocable. No sunset on either obligation. The perpetual IP assignment survives any NDA termination — meaning even if we exit the accelerator programme, Momentum retains its assignment rights.

No compelled disclosure carve-out. Section 4.2 states: 'Company shall not disclose any Program Information pursuant to any legal process without prior written consent of Momentum Ventures Studio.' Consent requirement for legal compulsion is unenforceable in court proceedings. Additionally, section 4.1 prohibits disclosure to 'any investor, acquirer, or third-party financier' without Momentum's consent — this would prevent us from running a fundraise while in the programme.
No return/destruction clause. Not the most critical gap given the other defects, but consistent with a document designed to accumulate and retain rights rather than discharge them.

Section 6.1 'Work for Hire': 'Any software, algorithm, model, or technical work created, improved, or adapted by Company during the Program Period shall be deemed a work made for hire pursuant to 17 U.S.C. §101, with Momentum Ventures Studio as the commissioning party.' Software created by company employees is not 'specially ordered or commissioned' within the §101 work-for-hire categories — but defending this position in litigation is expensive. Section 6.2: Momentum pre-consented to seek 'all available relief including specific performance and IP transfer orders' for any breach.

California law. Courts of the State of California (Santa Clara County). While California is acceptable for a technology company, Cal. Lab. Code §2870 voids IP assignments for inventions developed on employee's own time using their own resources — the accelerator's assignment clause does not carve out §2870-protected employee inventions, creating a potential conflict between the NDA's IP assignment and individual employee rights.

Document structured as one-way with us as Receiving Party. However, we are the technology owner disclosing to the accelerator — not the standard NDA Receiving Party. The Receiving Party designation in this document is inverted relative to economic reality: we are the ones exposing IP, but the document places IP assignment obligations on us as 'Receiving Party.'

No exclusions from confidentiality. All information exchanged is permanently confidential with no public domain, prior knowledge, independent development, or third-party disclosure carve-outs. Combined with the IP assignment clause, this means any technology we independently develop that Momentum could claim was 'discussed during the Program' is both confidential AND assigned to Momentum.

Not only missing — section 5.2 contains an affirmative present-tense IP assignment: 'Company hereby irrevocably assigns to Momentum Ventures Studio all right, title, and interest (including patent, copyright, and trade secret rights) in any improvement, adaptation, or derivative work of the Disclosed Technology.' This is the most dangerous clause in this document. 'Hereby assigns' is a present-tense transfer executed at signing.

critical_flags: CL-001: Oral disclosures without written confirmation — every pitch meeting conversation is captured indefinitely
CL-002: Document inverts Receiving Party designation — IP assignment obligations flow against us as disclosing party
CL-003: Perpetual IP assignment irrevocable — survives programme exit and NDA termination
CL-004: Investor/fundraise disclosure requires Momentum consent — prevents independent fundraising
CL-006: Section 6.1 work-for-hire clause — attempts to claim software created during programme
CL-010: Section 5.2 present-tense IP assignment ('hereby assigns') executed at NDA signing
recommended_redlines: CL-ALL: Reject this document entirely. Do not counter-propose. Draft and send own mutual NDA with no IP provisions, 3-year term, and California law.
CL-001: If any NDA is signed, require written confirmation of oral disclosures within 5 days
CL-004: Delete consent requirement for investor disclosure. Insert standard compelled disclosure carve-out.
CL-006: Delete work-for-hire provision in its entirety. No accelerator NDA should contain this clause.
CL-010: Delete section 5.2 IP assignment in its entirety. Insert standard no-license clause.
CL-009: Insert standard four exclusions before any engagement
